electric_field_direction: Which candidate arrow shows the electric field direction at P?
At point P, choose the option arrow that matches the net electric field direction.
Using the signs and positions of the charges, which labeled arrow gives the electric field direction at P?
Which option A-H points in the direction of the electric field at P?
Infer the net field direction at P. Which candidate arrow represents it?
force_on_positive_charge: Which candidate arrow shows the force direction on the positive test charge at P?
At point P, choose the option arrow that matches the force on the positive test charge.
Using the signs and positions of the charges, which labeled arrow gives the force direction on the positive test charge at P?
Which option A-H points in the direction of the force on the positive test charge at P?
Infer the force on the positive test charge at P. Which candidate arrow represents it?
force_on_negative_charge: Which candidate arrow shows the force direction on the negative test charge at P?
At point P, choose the option arrow that matches the force on the negative test charge.
Using the signs and positions of the charges, which labeled arrow gives the force direction on the negative test charge at P?
Which option A-H points in the direction of the force on the negative test charge at P?
Infer the force on the negative test charge at P. Which candidate arrow represents it?
zero_field_point_label: Which labeled point A-F has zero net electric field?
Using the shown charge strengths and positions, which candidate point has zero electric field?
Choose the labeled point where the electric fields from the fixed charges cancel.
Among points A-F, which one is the zero-field point?
Which candidate point is located where the net electric field is zero?
potential_value: Using k=1 and the shown distances, what is the signed electric potential at P?
Compute the electric potential at P from the visible charges and distances, with k=1.
What signed integer value gives V at P when V=sum(q/r)?
Using the displayed q and r labels, determine the signed potential at P.
Read the field map and compute the electric potential at P with k=1.

scene:electrostatic_field_map.object_description: an electrostatic coordinate grid with fixed point charges, a marked point or labeled candidate points, and visible option arrows or distance labels

query:electric_field_direction.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "Q1", "Q2", "Q3", and "P"; each value is the [x,y] pixel point at the center of that marker
query:electric_field_direction.answer_hint: set "answer" to the option letter of the candidate arrow

query:force_on_positive_charge.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "Q1", "Q2", "Q3", and "P"; each value is the [x,y] pixel point at the center of that marker
query:force_on_positive_charge.answer_hint: set "answer" to the option letter of the candidate arrow

query:force_on_negative_charge.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "Q1", "Q2", "Q3", and "P"; each value is the [x,y] pixel point at the center of that marker
query:force_on_negative_charge.answer_hint: set "answer" to the option letter of the candidate arrow

query:zero_field_point_label.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "Q1", "Q2", and "zero_point"; each value is the [x,y] pixel point at the center of that marker
query:zero_field_point_label.answer_hint: set "answer" to the option letter of the labeled zero-field point

query:potential_value.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "Q1", "Q2", "Q3", and "P"; each value is the [x,y] pixel point at the center of that marker
query:potential_value.answer_hint: set "answer" to the signed integer electric potential at P
